commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: make setup script template-friendly (#46)

## Why

This repo serves as both a working example and the primary template for
MCP server development on mctx.ai. When someone creates a project from
this template, the setup script left behind mctx-internal artifacts —
private GitHub Actions, bot credentials, stale CODEOWNERS — that caused
CI failures and confusion for template users.

## What This Does

The setup script now strips mctx-specific content and guides users
through configuring their own release workflow. It deletes private
action workflows (pr-comment, pr-title, dependabot-auto-merge), prompts
for bot identity to configure the release workflow, handles CODEOWNERS
(prompt to set up or remove), strips the hardcoded homepage, replaces
test files when using the minimal skeleton, and adds a comprehensive
RELEASE.md documenting how to set up the dual-branch release process
with a GitHub App or PAT.

---
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code)

Match header call button size to nav dropdown buttons

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

sdr: switch SSTV to slowrx-cli (headless, no GTK), bump to 1.0.1

slowrx (windytan) requires GTK3 — can't build headless.
slowrx-cli (sgarriga) is a CLI-only fork with no display dependency.
Outputs BMP; Pillow converts to PNG.

HF audio rate corrected to 16000 Hz (2048000/128) for clean decimation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix audio practice: BPM rate correct, beat offset bug fixed, OOM fix, sample rate resample, safe area

C++ metronome: beat offset (beatDisplacementFrames_) was added to every nextBeatFrame_ in render(),
making effective BPM = sampleRate*60/(fpb+offset) instead of the target BPM. Fixed: offset applied
once at start() as initial nextBeatFrame_. Nudge now uses pendingPhaseShift_ atomic (applied once
at next beat boundary, then cleared). AudioEngine::nudgeClick updated to use addPhaseShift().

AppViewModel: OOM fix (mutableListOf<Short> boxes PCM → ByteArray chunks + ShortBuffer primitives).
44.1→48kHz linear interpolation resample in decodeAudio. applyEngineSettings skips metadata BPM
if detectedBpm>0 (analysis result drives click). applySpeed uses detectedBpm as base if set.
Unified transport: play()/pause()/stop() + toggleClickMute(). changeClickGain/changeTrackGain.

AudioEngineBridge: nativeGetSampleRate() external.

GigBooksApp: safeDrawingPadding on NavHost, statusBarsPadding on DrawerHeader (edge-to-edge fix).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: phase 10 — production readiness (#11)

* feat: phase 10 — production readiness

- CI/CD: .github/workflows/deploy.yml — Docker multi-arch build (amd64+arm64),
  push to GHCR on push to main/master or semver tag; lint+test gate + step summary
- Graceful shutdown: uvicorn --timeout-graceful-shutdown 10 in Dockerfile CMD
- Structured logging: src/logging_config.py using structlog; JSON by default
  (LOG_FORMAT=json), coloured text in dev (LOG_FORMAT=text); main.py and db/init.py
  updated to use structlog.get_logger() with contextual key-value pairs
- Migration tracking: platform.schema_migration table (db/011_migration_tracking.sql);
  bootstrap() records filename + SHA-256 checksum; skips already-applied migrations
- Query Workbench: POST /api/v1/query (SELECT-only, role-scoped layer access, 500-row cap)
  + GET /api/v1/query/tables; QueryWorkbenchPage.tsx with SQL editor
  (Tab→indent, Ctrl+Enter→run), table browser sidebar, results table, chart toggle
- Docs: CLAUDE.md + roadmap.md updated to reflect Phase 10 complete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove unused api import in QueryWorkbenchPage, use getToken()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add fc range to DownloadProgress log messages

Log format now shows actual frame counter range from received data:
"DownloadProgress: 8/75 (L2CAP) (fc:1200-1207)"
Appends " *" if a reboot occurred within the interval (fc wrapped).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify UnaryLogicalNot → §11.4.7 [skip ci]

The unary logical NOT operator (!) is defined in §11.4.7 "Logical operators", which covers unary logical negation alongside &&, ||, ->, and <->. The test parses `!a` and checks that the resulting expression has kind kUnary with op kBang, directly exercising this operator's syntax.

feat(grpc-removal): remove gRPC service definitions from proto files (Phase 5.3) (#28)

* feat(grpc-removal): remove gRPC service definitions from proto files (Phase 5.3)

Remove all `service` blocks and RPC method definitions from:
- validator.proto (BeaconNodeValidator service, 24 RPCs)
- beacon_chain.proto (BeaconChain service, 13 RPCs)
- node.proto (Node service, 10 RPCs)
- health.proto (Health service, 1 RPC)

Also removes unused imports (google/api/annotations.proto,
google/protobuf/empty.proto) that were only referenced by service
definitions. All message definitions are preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(grpc-removal): remove StreamBlocksAltair dead code from beacon-api (Phase 5.3)

Remove stream_blocks.go, its tests, and the StreamBlocksAltair method
from beacon_api_validator_client.go. These reference the generated
ethpb.BeaconNodeValidator_StreamBlocksAltairClient type which will no
longer exist after proto service definitions are regenerated.
StreamBlocksAltair is not part of the iface.ValidatorClient interface,
making all of this code dead.

commit.message:
Feat: add hook system (#9)

* Feat: add hook system with logging

Run shell commands on profile events (e.g. profile_loaded).
Hooks are configured in ringo.toml as [[hooks]] entries.
Commands run in background threads with stdout/stderr logged
to /tmp/ringo-hooks.log.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Docs: document hooks in README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Feat: add unified logging module with file output

Introduces src/log.rs with OnceLock<Mutex<File>>, writing to
/tmp/ringo-{profile}.log. Replaces ad-hoc logging in hooks.rs and
surfaces previously silent errors across TCP, config parsing, baresip
lifecycle, call history, dial history, notify-send, and phone commands.

* Docs: update README for unified log file

Hooks now fire on incoming and outgoing calls with event data
(call_id, number, display_name) passed as RINGO_EVENT_DATA JSON
and individual RINGO_<KEY> env vars.

* Feat: add call_ended hook event

Fires when a call closes with event data: call_id, number, direction,
duration_secs, reason, and error flag.

Fix stale _movedThisTick flag blocking window boundary movements

The single-hop-per-tick guard was not being cleared at the start of
each tick, causing window boundary operations (walks, scatter, returns)
to be blocked by the stale flag from the previous tick's planner moves.

Added early reset at tick start so WB processing runs unrestricted,
followed by a second reset before the coordinated move planner.

Move one-mark upload trigger after recordStop in sliceRecording

to complete before triggering the upload.

Sin esto, Supabase solo devolvía 1000 filas y podía crear duplicados
en mesa_asignaciones y resultados al no detectar las existentes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make recurring mode internet-independent (local-first advancement)

Previously, recurring restarts required a successful write to Firebase on every
cycle via fetch("/api/broadcast"). A 1-second network blip at cycle-end would
permanently kill the session.

Two fixes in display/page.tsx:

1. Local-first advancement: the recurring setTimeout now advances the display
   state immediately using nextStart = currentStart + totalMs (pure math, no
   network). The API call is fired in the background for syncing other devices
   but its failure no longer affects this display.

2. Recurring catch-up in onValue: when Firebase delivers stale data on
   reconnect (e.g. after flight mode), the early-expiry guard now computes
   the current round number (n = floor(elapsed / totalMs)) and jumps to the
   correct round instead of resetting to idle. The display can recover from
   arbitrarily long offline periods with a single Firebase packet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add v5 status report (inline script extraction, Instagram fix)

Overall score 7.9 -> 8.1, Performance 8.5 -> 9, Code Quality 7.5 -> 8.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify UnaryMinus → §11.4.3 [skip ci]

Table 11-6 in §11.4.3 "Arithmetic operators" explicitly defines the unary minus (−m) operator. There is no more specific subclause for unary arithmetic operators; §11.4.3.1 covers signed/unsigned typing, not the operator definition itself.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add batch speaker avatar endpoint for network graph

Single POST /api/v1/images/speakers/batch replaces 100+ concurrent image
requests with one batch fetch. Uses 20-thread pool for parallel S3 reads,
prefers pre-generated 100x100 minis (~16KB each). 100 speakers in ~250ms,
500 in ~600ms. Fixes broken image loading in speaker network graph.

commit.message:
fix: align workout dates to correct ISO week days when plan starts mid-week

_next_weekday_from assumed reference was always Monday, but plan_start
is date.today() which can be any weekday. Fix by computing the Monday
of the reference week before adding the preferred-day offset.

commit.message:
Phase 12: Social features (no database, localStorage only)

Added community page with 4 tabs:

1. Mon profil
   - Custom username + bio
   - Emoji avatar selector
   - Profile statistics (sessions, friends count)
   - Save/load with localStorage

2. Amis (Friend system)
   - Add friends by friend code
   - Display friend list with avatars
   - Remove friends
   - Friend count tracking

3. Partager (Share programs)
   - Select program to share
   - Generate shareable link with encoded program
   - Copy link to clipboard
   - Share code uses Base64 encoding

4. Feed (Activity feed)
   - Display friend activities
   - Track program shares
   - Timeline view

Storage:
- Profiles: localStorage 'mos-profile'
- Friends list: localStorage 'mos-friends'
- Share codes: URL query params (no DB needed)

Features:
- Tab navigation with active states
- Responsive grid layout
- Emoji avatars
- Friend code system
- Link generation and copying
- CSS animations for tab transitions
- Mobile-optimized UI

Files modified:
- index.html: Added page-community with 4 tabs
- js/app.js: Added Phase 12 functions (saveProfile, addFriend, generateShareLink, etc.)
- css/components.css: Added community tab styles, friend cards, feed items

Pure localStorage implementation - no backend required
Works completely offline

commit.message:
feat: replace SQLite with PostgreSQL, fix slow dashboard (#10)

* feat: replace SQLite with PostgreSQL and fix slow dashboard loading

Migrate from better-sqlite3 to pg (node-postgres) with a connection pool.
All route handlers and orchestrator code converted to async. Also fixes
~10s skeleton flash on dashboard load by making gh CLI calls non-blocking
(execFileSync → execFileAsync + Promise.all) and warming the orchestrator
state cache on startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR #10 review feedback

1. Wrap SSE send() in try/catch to prevent unhandled promise rejections
2. Wrap projects POST/PUT multi-insert operations in transactions
3. Remove unused execFileSync import (convert recoverStaleJobs to async)
4. Move const declaration after all import statements
5. Use RETURNING * in UPDATE routes instead of separate SELECT queries
